title: RRSPs and related plans - Canada.ca
content:
## RRSPs and related plans
The following links provide information for annuitants, contributors, or beneficiaries of registered plans for retirement, saving for your first home, buying or building a qualified home, or continuing education.
Plan administrators should consult Savings and pension plan administration .
- Definitions for RRSPs
- Registered Retirement Savings Plan (RRSP) Setting up, contributing, transferring, and claiming deductions for plans for yourself or your spouse or common-law partner
- RRSP options when you turn 71 Your options for your RRSP when you turn 71
- How to purchase an advanced life deferred annuity (ALDA) Option to defer your RRSP or RRIF until age 85
- First Home Savings Account (FHSA) Information about saving for your first home tax-free (up to certain limits).
- The Home Buyers' Plan Withdrawing funds from your RRSPs to buy or build a qualified home
- Lifelong Learning Plan Withdrawing funds from your RRSPs for continuing education or retraining
- Registered Retirement Income Fund (RRIF) Setting up, transferring other types of funds, getting and reporting income, and tax implications when the annuitant of a RRIF dies, measure for annuitants of RRIF and changes to the conversion age
- Anti-avoidance rules for RRSPs and RRIFs Anti-avoidance rules are strengthened to prevent aggressive tax planning
- Important dates Important dates for RRSP, HBP, LLP, FHSAs and more
- Pooled Registered Pension Plan (PRPP) Joining, contributing, transferring, withdrawing, life events, and investment choices
- Registered Education Savings Plan

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
